title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
Proxy wraps an object and intercepts fundamental operations on it (property access, assignment, function calls, etc.) using handler traps.

Reflect is a built-in object that provides methods mirroring the Proxy traps. It allows you to perform the default operation inside a trap without re-implementing it manually.

Common Proxy traps:
• get(target, prop) — intercept property reads.
• set(target, prop, value) — intercept property writes.
• has(target, prop) — intercept the in operator.
• deleteProperty / apply / construct — and more.

Use cases: validation, logging, virtual properties, reactive data systems (Vue 3 uses Proxy for reactivity).
